title: Effect of Tamsulosin in Prevention of Postoperative Urinary Retention
journal_citation: (May issue) (Pakistan Journal of Health Sciences, 2026: Volume 07 Issue 5
summary: Evaluates perioperative tamsulosin as a preventive strategy for postoperative urinary retention. The work supports risk reduction in patients vulnerable to urinary retention after surgery.

title: Safety And Effectiveness of -Adrenergic Blockers For Pediatric Urolithiasis In The Distal Ureter: A Systematic Review
journal_citation: (World Journal of Advanced Research and Reviews, Vol. 25, Issue No. 02 – February 2025)
summary: Reviews alpha-adrenergic blockers as medical expulsive therapy for distal ureteric stones in children. It weighs stone passage benefits against safety concerns in pediatric urolithiasis.

title: Assessment of Necessity of Retrograde Filling during PCNL Tract Dilatation
journal_citation: (Journal of Bahria University Medical & Dental College, Vol. 15 Issue No. 02; 2025)
summary: Assesses whether retrograde filling is required during tract dilatation for percutaneous nephrolithotomy. The study is relevant to simplifying PCNL technique while maintaining procedural safety.

title: Efficacy of Tamsulosin Versus silodosin in Patients with Lower Ureteric Calculi
journal_citation: (Journal of Frontier in Medical & Health Research Vol. 3, Issue No. 02, 2025)
issue_note: Link not found not in the issue direct to frontier journol websioter
summary: Compares tamsulosin and silodosin for medical expulsive therapy in lower ureteric stones. The paper focuses on improving spontaneous stone passage and symptom control.

title: Comparative Analysis of Mini Percutaneous Nephrolithotomy Versus Standard Percutaneous Nephrolithotomy In Terms of Stone Free Rates, Hospital Stay And Post-Operative Complications In Pediatric Population: A Systematic Review
journal_citation: (Gomal Journal of Medical Sciences October-December 2024, Vol. 22, No. 4)
summary: Compares mini-PCNL and standard PCNL in children, focusing on stone-free rates, hospital stay, and complications. The review helps frame procedure selection for pediatric renal stone disease.

title: Efficacy of Retrograde Intrarenal Surgery Versus Percutaneous Nephrolithotomy In Treating Lower Pole Stones Of 1-2 CMS
summary: Compares retrograde intrarenal surgery and PCNL for 1–2 cm lower pole renal stones. The article addresses treatment effectiveness and decision-making for moderate-sized lower pole calculi.

title: Determinants of Partial Stone Clearance after Mini-PCNL in the Pediatric Population
journal_citation: (National Journal of Health Sciences, 2024, Vol. 9 No. 01, 23-28)
summary: Identifies factors linked with incomplete stone clearance after mini-PCNL in children. The study supports better preoperative counseling and follow-up planning for pediatric stone surgery.

title: Wait and Watch: Management Strategies of Multi Cystic Dysplastic Kidney in Pediatric Population
summary: Discusses conservative observation strategies for multicystic dysplastic kidney in children. It highlights when careful follow-up may be appropriate instead of immediate intervention.

title: The Penile Fracture, Surgical Outcome and Post-operative Complications
summary: Reviews surgical management outcomes for penile fracture and tracks postoperative complications. The work emphasizes timely repair and functional recovery after urological trauma.

title: Effectiveness of Double J Stent with Extracorporeal Shockwave Lithotripsy: An Analysis of Stone-Free Rates
summary: Analyzes whether double-J stenting with ESWL improves stone-free outcomes. The study is useful for selecting patients who may benefit from adjunctive stenting.

title: Acute Kidney Injury in Patients Presenting with Severe Covid-19 - A Descriptive Study.
summary: Describes acute kidney injury patterns among patients presenting with severe COVID-19. The study adds local clinical data on renal complications during severe infection.

title: Effectiveness of Extracorporeal Shockwave Lithotripsy in Removing Small and Large Stones.
journal_citation: (P J M H S Vol. 17, No. 04, April, 2023)
summary: Evaluates ESWL performance across different urinary stone sizes. The article helps clarify stone-size selection when considering non-invasive lithotripsy.

title: Role of miRNA In The Diagnosis And Progression Of Prostate Cancer Among Young Age Male
summary: Explores microRNA as a diagnostic and progression marker for prostate cancer in younger men. It connects molecular biomarkers with early detection and risk assessment.

title: Involution of Unilateral Multicystic Dysplastic Kidney: Long Term Data From A Single Center.
summary: Reports long-term follow-up of unilateral multicystic dysplastic kidney and its natural involution. The study supports evidence-based observation in selected pediatric renal cases.

title: Day Care Percutaneous Nephrolithotomy in COVID Era
journal_citation: (Journal of Rehman Medical Institute, Vol 9, No. 2, 2023.)
summary: Evaluates day-care PCNL feasibility during the COVID era to reduce hospital exposure. The paper highlights safe stone surgery pathways under constrained healthcare conditions.

title: Experience of Physical Examination as a Primary Tool for Surgical Access Planning in Hemodialysis Patients
journal_citation: (National Journal of Health Sciences, 2023, Vol. 8. No. 2.)
summary: Assesses physical examination as a practical first-line method for hemodialysis access planning. It supports low-cost clinical assessment before reliance on advanced imaging.

title: Mini-PCNL in Pediatric Patients with Large Renal Stones- A Safety Data.
summary: Presents safety data for mini-PCNL in children with large renal stones. The study supports minimally invasive stone clearance while monitoring pediatric surgical risk.

title: Effect of Mirabegron to Reduce Lower Urinary Tract Symptoms in Prostatitis.
journal_citation: (P J M H S Vol. 16, No. 09, September, 2022)
summary: Studies mirabegron for reducing storage-related lower urinary tract symptoms in prostatitis. The work focuses on symptom relief and quality-of-life improvement.

title: Innovative Way To Break The Stone: Master Lithoclast For Bilateral Simultaneous Percutaneous Nephrolithotomy
summary: Describes use of the Master Lithoclast during bilateral simultaneous PCNL. The article highlights an efficient stone-fragmentation approach for complex bilateral renal calculi.

title: Hydroperitoneum: A Complication Of Mini PCNL In Pediatric Age Group? -A Case Report.
journal_citation: (Liaquat Medical Research Journal LMRJ Volume 3 Issue 4 30- DEC-2021)
summary: Reports hydroperitoneum as a possible complication of pediatric mini-PCNL. The case reinforces vigilance, early recognition, and management of uncommon postoperative events.

title: Simulator Based Teaching of Trans-Urethral Resection of Prostate
summary: Evaluates simulator-based training for transurethral resection of prostate skills. The paper supports structured simulation as a safer way to build urology operative competence.

title: Comparing Non-Stented, Stented, and Intra Ureteral Bupivacaine Instillation Treatment After Ureteroscopic Stone Fragmentation
summary: Compares postoperative strategies after ureteroscopic stone fragmentation, including stenting and intra-ureteral bupivacaine. The study focuses on pain control and recovery after ureteroscopy.

title: Outcome of Single Optical Internal Urethrotomy In Naïve Urethra In Young Population
summary: Evaluates outcomes of single optical internal urethrotomy in young patients with untreated urethral stricture. The paper helps define success expectations after first-line endoscopic treatment.

title: Antibiotic Prophylaxis Before Cystoscopy in Female Patients: A Prospective Comparative Study
summary: Compares antibiotic prophylaxis strategies before cystoscopy in female patients. The study informs infection-prevention decisions for common diagnostic urology procedures.

title: Pain During Neonatal Circumcision: Comparison in Dorsal Penile Versus Combined Dorsal and Ventral Penile Block In A Randomized Control Trial
summary: Compares dorsal penile block with combined dorsal and ventral block for neonatal circumcision analgesia. The randomized trial focuses on improving pain control during a common pediatric procedure.

title: Age Specific Outcome of Single Stage Orchidopexy In Pediatric Population: Prospective Comparative Study
summary: Studies age-specific outcomes after single-stage orchidopexy in children. The article supports timing and counseling decisions for undescended testis surgery.

title: Efficacy of Percutaneous Nephrolithotomy (PCNL) In Previously Operated
journal_citation: (Pylo /Nephrolithotomy) With Recurrent Renal Stones. (7):13938-13941.) (Ind Am J Pharm Sci.2019; 6
summary: Assesses PCNL efficacy in patients with recurrent renal stones after previous renal surgery. The study is relevant to complex stone management in surgically altered anatomy.

title: Frequency and detection of risk factors of urinary bladder tumor in rural population of Sindh province
summary: Identifies frequency and risk factors for urinary bladder tumors in rural Sindh. The study adds regional epidemiologic insight for earlier detection and prevention strategies.

title: Haemodialysis requirement in patients with malarial acute kidney injury
summary: Measures how often hemodialysis is needed in malarial acute kidney injury. The work highlights renal severity and supportive care needs in malaria-related AKI.

title: Outcomes of open versus endoscopic procedures during vesicle calculus management
summary: Compares open and endoscopic approaches for vesical calculus management. The article focuses on clinical outcomes that guide procedure selection for bladder stones.

title: Success and recurrence rate of Optical Urethrotomy in management of anterior urethral stricture in male
summary: Analyzes success and recurrence after optical urethrotomy for anterior urethral stricture in men. The study helps define long-term expectations after endoscopic stricture treatment.

title: Determine the Outcome of Radical Cystectomy in Management of Muscle Invasive Bladder Tumor.
summary: Reviews outcomes of radical cystectomy for muscle-invasive bladder tumor. The paper contributes institutional experience on definitive surgical management of advanced bladder cancer.

title: The Age Distribution and Effectiveness of Low Dose Tamsolosin In Lower Ureteric Stones
summary: Examines age distribution and low-dose tamsulosin effectiveness for lower ureteric stones. The article supports medical expulsive therapy in selected stone patients.

title: Frequency of success of percutaneous nephrolithotomy (PCNL) procedures in patients with renal calculi
summary: Measures the success frequency of PCNL in patients with renal calculi. The study reinforces PCNL as a core intervention for larger or complex kidney stones.

title: Prostatic cancer in patients presenting with lower urinary track symptoms
summary: Studies prostate cancer occurrence among patients presenting with lower urinary tract symptoms. The article emphasizes evaluation of LUTS beyond benign prostate disease.

title: Determine the frequency of hepatorenal syndrome in patients with cirrhosis associated with Hepatitis C
summary: Determines hepatorenal syndrome frequency in hepatitis C-related cirrhosis. The work links chronic liver disease with renal dysfunction risk and clinical monitoring needs.

title: Effect of Age and gender on the clinicopathological features of bladder tumor
summary: Examines how age and gender relate to clinicopathological patterns of bladder tumors. The study helps describe demographic influences on tumor presentation and behavior.

title: Serum Electrolyte Disturbances in benign prostate hyperplasia after transurethral resection of the prostate
summary: Analyzes serum electrolyte disturbances after TURP for benign prostatic hyperplasia. The article highlights postoperative monitoring needs, especially for fluid and sodium imbalance.

title: Renal Osteodystrophy in End Stage Renal Failure Patients on Maintenance Haemodialysis
summary: Studies renal osteodystrophy among end-stage renal failure patients on maintenance hemodialysis. The work emphasizes bone-mineral complications in chronic kidney disease care.

title: Chemical Analysis of Urinary Stones, Composition and its locations Associated To Urinary Tract.
summary: Analyzes urinary stone composition and its association with stone location in the urinary tract. The study supports prevention planning through metabolic and chemical stone profiling.

title: Management and Outcome of Bracka’s Procedure on Penile Shaft Hypospadias.
summary: Reviews management and outcomes of Bracka’s procedure for penile shaft hypospadias. The article contributes surgical outcome data for complex pediatric reconstructive urology.

title: Early Removal of 3 Way Foleys Catheter After Transurethral Resection of Prostate Is Beneficial?
journal_citation: (Med. Forum 2012 March, Vol 23, No.3, Page No. 41)
summary: Evaluates whether early removal of a three-way Foley catheter after TURP is beneficial. The study focuses on recovery, catheter duration, and postoperative patient comfort.
